title: phaseolus vulgaris var. vinosus
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. vinosus, also known as purple or wine-colored kidney bean, is a variety of the common bean plant, which is a type of legume native to the Americas. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as arthritis and improving cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Phaseolus vulgaris var. vinosus include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Traditional herbal preparations include decoctions and infusions made from the leaves and stems of the plant, which can be consumed as tea or used topically to soothe skin irritations.
